2605.06283.title: Quantifying the Statistical Effect of Rubric Modifications on Human-Autorater Agreement
2605.06283.paper_summary: Autoraters, also referred to as LLM-as-judges, are increasingly used for evaluation and automated content moderation. However, there is limited statistical analysis of how modifications in a rubric presented to both humans and autoraters affect their score agreement. Rubrics that ask for an overall or \emph{holistic} judgment - for example, rating the ``quality'' of an essay - may be inconsistently interpreted due to the complexity or subjectivity of the criteria. Conversely, rubrics can ask for \emph{analytic} judgments, which decompose assessment criteria - for example, ``quality'' into ``fluency'' and ``organization''. While these rubrics can be edited to improve the individual accuracy of both human and automated scoring, this approach may result in disagreement between the two scores, or with the associated holistic judgment. Designing and deploying reliable autoraters requires understanding not just the relationship between human and autorater annotations but how that relationship changes as holistic or analytic judgments are elicited. The results indicate that rubric edits providing representative examples and additional context, and reducing positional bias in the rubric increased human-autorater agreement, while higher rubric complexity and conservative aggregation methods tended to decrease it. The findings from the automatic essay scoring and instruction-following evaluation domains suggest that practitioners should carefully analyze domain- and rubric-specific performance to move towards higher human-autorater agreement.
2605.06283.paper_summary_zh: 評分系統，也被稱為 LLM-as-judges，越來越多地用於評估和自動內容審核。<lb/>然而，對於呈現給人類和評分系統的評分標準的修改如何影響其分數一致性，進行的統計分析有限。<lb/>要求整體或 \emph{整體} 判斷的評分標準——例如，評價一篇論文的“質量”——可能因標準的複雜性或主觀性而被不一致地解釋。<lb/>相反，評分標準可以要求 \emph{分析性} 判斷，這些判斷將評估標準分解——例如，“質量”分解為“流暢性”和“組織性”。<lb/>雖然這些評分標準可以進行編輯以提高人類和自動評分的個別準確性，但這種方法可能導致兩個分數之間的分歧，或與相關的整體判斷不一致。<lb/>設計和部署可靠的評分系統需要理解人類和評分系統註釋之間的關係，以及當引出整體或分析性判斷時該關係如何變化。<lb/>結果表明，提供代表性示例和額外上下文的評分標準編輯，以及減少評分標準中的位置偏見，增加了人類與評分系統的一致性，而較高的評分標準複雜性和保守的聚合方法則傾向於降低一致性。<lb/>來自自動論文評分和遵循指令評估領域的研究結果表明，實踐者應仔細分析特定領域和評分標準的表現，以朝著更高的人類與評分系統一致性邁進。
2605.06283.author: Jessica Huynh et.al.
2605.06283.authors: Jessica Huynh, Alfredo Gomez, Athiya Deviyani, Renee Shelby, Jeffrey P. Bigham, Fernando Diaz
